items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: OpenAI models are already escaping their sandboxes during benchmarks and finding exploits in other infrastructure and just doing whatever. Majority of the world’s infrastructures are not ready for this level of damage at large scale if an AI decides the wrong thing. Imagine hospitals and banks.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: Back in 2010 getting indie game sales reports for Vizati by physical mail. “I wonder how many copies I sold …. oh”. It was a rough indie dev start but it changed my career, had to do a custom engine for mobile version (which sold a bit better) and later used the engine on QoD.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: Usually criticized by "not innovating as much" as the competition/using older tech, I think Apple approach of waiting and see here, might pay off as the other companies bury themselves in debt. Nintendo also tries to use established tech but in fun/different ways, it works.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: It’s weird but for Switch 1 I was 80% of the time in portable mode, I found the joycons detached uncomfortable so Pro Controller on TV. Switch 2 I find portable mode uncomfortable so 99% time docked, but since joycons are bigger they are more comfortable so that’s what I use.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: 16GB of SO-DIMM DDR5 RAM, something you can use on a Steam Machine, was around 36 Euros one year ago, in May 2025 (lowest ever). This particular one was at it's peak 219 Euros this March. This alone would account for a big increase on SM price, let alone everything else.

items.title: 无标题文章
items.summary: I wonder how popular a "buy the game on a Disc" service on GOG would be? The games are already DRM-free, on checkout one could tick a box and get a physical delivery for games in the cart. Obviously this would be a logistics nightmare to burn on demand, but a nice feature.

items.summary: They idea of building a DIY mini-pc and throwing SteamOS in there is tempting, but even with mini cases they don't get as close, the most interesting aspect of the Steam Machine is still incredibly tiny size and silent (from what I read about) image src: https://www. reddit.com/r/steammachine/comm ents/1unos12/steam_machine_vs_15_consoles_size_comparison/
